name: instant-api — backup.requested with no follow-up within 30m [stuck backup]
description: Warn when backups are requested but not completing. NR alert conditions cannot run subqueries, so this cannot do a true per-backup_id A-without-B join. Instead it measures the rate gap inside each aggregation window: count(backup.requested) minus count(backup.succeeded + backup.failed). A sustained positive gap means requests are piling up faster than the worker finishes them — the same symptom a stuck backup produces (worker crashed mid-snapshot). The 30m aggregation window roughly matches the per-row 30m backup timeout in worker/internal/jobs/customer_backup_runner.go, so a single stuck backup surfaces within one window. KNOWN LIMITATION: this is population-level, not per-id — a healthy burst that straddles a window boundary can show a transient gap, hence the multi-occurrence terms. FOLLOW-UP (preferred fix): add a worker reconciler that emits a `backup.stuck` audit event when a resource_backups row sits in 'pending'/'running' past the 30m timeout; the alert then becomes a trivial `SELECT count(*) FROM Log WHERE audit_kind = 'backup.stuck'` with exact per-backup precision. Source: audit events from api/internal/handlers/backup.go (backup.requested) and worker/internal/jobs/backup_audit.go (backup.succeeded / backup.failed).

nrql.query: SELECT filter(count(*), WHERE audit_kind = 'backup.requested') - filter(count(*), WHERE audit_kind IN ('backup.succeeded', 'backup.failed')) AS uncompleted_backups FROM Log WHERE service = 'api' AND audit_kind IN ('backup.requested', 'backup.succeeded', 'backup.failed')
